title: The McDonald's of Psychedelics Is Here
text: This week in our Hypergrowth Investing podcast , we're talking about my Big Three investing themes for the rest of the year (and beyond). You've probably heard me talk about each of these at one point or another.

title: McDonald's is fighting a California bill that would support fast food workers
text: California lawmakers want more control over how fast food chains treat workers. Restaurants, including McDonald's, are fighting back.

title: McDonald's US president says California's newly created fast-food council that could raise minimum wage to $22 at its restaurants 'hurts everyone'
text: The California bill, known as the FAST Recovery Act, would create a 10-person council that can set safety standards and raise the minimum wage to $22.

title: McDonald's U.S. head says California fast-food bill unfairly targets big chains
text: McDonald's U.S. President Joe Erlinger on Wednesday criticized a landmark California bill that would give the state more control over pay for fast-food workers.

title: California's senate just passed a 'fast food council' law that could force McDonald's, Starbucks, and other chains to raise wages up to $22 an hour
text: Labor advocates say the bill will give workers a voice, while opponents say it could raise prices.

title: 3 Safe Growth Stocks to Buy for Long-Lasting Dividends
text: Safe growth stocks with dividends are appealing because of their stability and predictability. Buying them will keep investors largely protected from volatility while also providing them with income and a good chance of realizing significant capital gains.

title: Jeff Bezos tweets a picture of himself eating a McDonald's burger in a throwback to his first job
text: The Amazon founder pays homage to the fast-food chain that gave him his first job and says it's "still the same great burger".

title: Should You Buy This Dividend Aristocrat for Growing Passive Income?

title: Breakfast sales hold steady as people heading back to offices cut back elsewhere

title: Coca-Cola, McDonald's and Tesco among the worst UK packaging polluters, survey shows
text: A dozen companies, including The Coca-Cola Company (NYSE:KO), McDonald's Corp and Tesco PLC (LSE:TSCO) are responsible for 70% of branded packaging pollution in the UK, a survey has revealed.  The top three polluters, Coca-Cola, PepsiCo (NASDAQ:PEP) and McDonald's are responsible for 38% of all branded pollution, according to a report by the charity Surfers Against Sewage.

title: Personalized coffees and prestige skincare: Consumers snap up premium products despite cost-of-living crisis

title: McDonald's Remains a Premium Defensive Play Despite McPlant Hiccup
text: McDonald's ( MCD , Financial) recently tested its McPlant burger in some restaurants. However, the test did not produce the desired results, as customers showed little interest in the product.

title: McDonald's is starting to reopen in Ukraine
text: After closing its restaurants in Ukraine six months ago because of the Russian invasion, McDonald's is starting to reopen in parts of the country.

title: McDonald's is reopening in Ukraine, where employees have 'a strong desire to return to work' and want a 'sense of normalcy'
text: McDonald's closed its restaurants in Ukraine after Russia invaded the country, but has continued paying over 10,000 Ukrainian McDonald's employees.

title: McDonald's set to reopen in some cities in Ukraine: Report
text: McDonald's Corp. is set to reopen some of its restaurants in Ukraine, according to a news report.

title: McDonald's says it's planning to reopen some restaurants in Ukraine
text: The fast-food giant said it will begin a phased re-opening of restaurants, beginning in Kyiv and western Ukraine.

title: McDonald's to begin reopening Ukraine restaurants
text: (Reuters) -McDonald's Corp said on Thursday it plans on reopening its restaurants in Ukraine in the coming months.

title: Food Prices Remain High. 4 Restaurant Stocks That Are Still Good Bets.

title: 7 Blue-Chip Stocks to Buy Now for Growth and Dividends
text: Investors looking for blue-chip stocks to buy generally are looking for companies that are reliable, consistent, profitable and focused on shareholder returns. Blue-chip stocks are less volatile than more speculative securities.
